items.headline: Knicks Just Two Wins Away from NBA Finals After Dominant Game 2!
items.summary: The New York Knicks took a commanding 2-0 series lead over the Cleveland Cavaliers in the Eastern Conference Finals with a 109-93 victory in Game 2 on Thursday night. This marks their ninth straight win, the longest postseason streak since the 2024 Celtics.

items.headline: Giannis Antetokounmpo at Center of Major Trade Rumors as Bucks Miss Playoffs
items.summary: After the Milwaukee Bucks missed the 2026 playoffs, two-time MVP Giannis Antetokounmpo is reportedly the most discussed name in trade rumors. Teams like the MIA Heat, NYK Knicks, MIN Timberwolves, and OKC Thunder are mentioned as potential suitors, with his contract extending through 2027-28.

items.headline: Thunder Take 2-1 Series Lead Over Spurs
items.summary: OKC's bench exploded for 76 points, leading the Thunder to a 123-108 victory over the San Antonio Spurs in Game 3 of the Western Conference Finals. OKC now leads the series 2-1.

items.headline: Knicks Dominate Cavs in Game 2
items.summary: The New York Knicks extended their Eastern Conference Finals lead to 2-0 with a convincing 109-93 win against the Cleveland Cavaliers.

items.headline: Josh Hart's Playoff Career-High
items.summary: Josh Hart exploded for a playoff career-high 26 points, including 5-of-11 from 3-point range, to lead the Knicks to their Game 2 victory. He also added 7 assists.

items.headline: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander Leads Thunder
items.summary: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander contributed 26 points and 12 assists, guiding the Thunder to their Game 3 win over the Spurs.

items.headline: Jared McCain's Playoff Career-Best
items.summary: Thunder rookie Jared McCain scored a playoff career-high 24 points off the bench in OKC's Game 3 victory.

items.headline: Deni Avdija's Playoff Contribution
items.summary: Earlier in the playoffs, Deni Avdija contributed 14 points, 4 rebounds, and 3 assists for the Trail Blazers against the Spurs in a first-round game, showcasing his all-around game.
items.statContext: Deni Avdija (POR) vs SAS (April 22, 2026 Playoff Game)

items.headline: Western Conference Finals Series Update: Thunder Lead 2-1
items.summary: The Oklahoma City Thunder have taken a 2-1 lead in the Western Conference Finals against the San Antonio Spurs after a dominant Game 3 performance, fueled by their bench. Game 4 is scheduled for Sunday, May 24th.

items.headline: De'Aaron Fox and Dylan Harper Battle Injuries for Spurs
items.summary: Spurs guard De'Aaron Fox reaggravated his right ankle injury in Game 3 but returned, while rookie Dylan Harper also played through an adductor injury. Both were available for Game 3.

items.headline: LeBron James Free Agency Decision Could Extend into August
items.summary: LeBron James, an unrestricted free agent, stated on his podcast that his decision on playing a 24th NBA season and his future destination is unlikely before late July or August.

items.headline: Lakers Eyeing Big Offseason Moves, Giannis Among Targets
items.summary: Rumors suggest the Lakers are looking to bolster their roster, with potential interest in Giannis Antetokounmpo. However, reports indicate it's unlikely LAL will have the best trade package for the Bucks superstar.

items.headline: Upcoming Game: Knicks at Cavaliers (Game 3) Tonight
items.summary: The Eastern Conference Finals continues tonight as the New York Knicks visit the Cleveland Cavaliers for Game 3 at 8:00 PM ET, with the Knicks leading the series 2-0.
